title: Trump Signs Executive Order to Establish National AI Policy, Overriding State Laws
summary: President Trump signed an executive order on December 11, 2025, creating a federal AI policy framework intended to preempt the patchwork of state AI regulations.
content: President Donald Trump signed an executive order titled 'Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence' on December 11, 2025, directing federal agencies to identify and challenge state AI laws that conflict with national innovation priorities. The order empowers the Justice Department to establish an AI Litigation Task Force to contest state regulations deemed unconstitutional or overly burdensome, while the Commerce Department will evaluate whether states' AI laws could jeopardize their eligibility for federal BEAD broadband funding. White House AI advisor David Sacks indicated the administration will focus on the most 'onerous' examples of state regulation, carving out safety-critical areas like child protection laws and state government AI procurement. The EO has already drawn opposition from a bipartisan coalition of 36 state attorneys general who sent a letter to Congress opposing any federal moratorium on state AI regulation, and the order is widely expected to face legal challenges. Companies should prepare for a transitional period of legal uncertainty as the administration targets laws like Colorado's algorithmic discrimination ban while the 90-day Commerce Department review of state AI laws gets underway.

title: OpenAI Report: Enterprise AI Adoption Is Accelerating Sharply, Workers Save 40–60 Minutes Daily
summary: OpenAI's first State of Enterprise AI report reveals explosive growth in workplace AI usage, with 75% of workers reporting improved speed or quality of output.
content: OpenAI released its inaugural State of Enterprise AI report on December 8, 2025, drawing on usage data from enterprise customers and a survey of 9,000 workers across nearly 100 companies. Weekly messages in ChatGPT Enterprise increased roughly 8× over the past year, while usage of structured workflows like Projects and Custom GPTs surged 19× year-to-date, signaling a decisive shift from casual experimentation to integrated, repeatable business processes. Workers report saving 40–60 minutes per day, with 87% of IT workers citing faster issue resolution, 73% of engineers reporting faster code delivery, and 75% of users saying AI enables them to complete entirely new tasks they previously could not perform. A widening gap is emerging between 'frontier' firms — those with deep, multi-team AI integration — and the median: frontier firms send 2× more messages per seat and show substantially higher productivity gains. OpenAI concludes that the primary constraints for enterprise AI are no longer model capability or tooling availability, but organizational readiness and implementation discipline.

title: OpenAI Codex Reaches General Availability with Slack Integration and Developer SDK
summary: OpenAI announced Codex is now generally available, adding a Slack integration, TypeScript SDK, and enterprise admin tools as daily usage has grown more than 10× since August.
content: OpenAI announced the general availability of Codex on October 6, 2025, marking the AI coding agent's transition from a May research preview into full production. The GA release introduces a Slack integration letting developers delegate coding tasks directly from team channels, a TypeScript Codex SDK for embedding the agent in custom engineering workflows, and new enterprise admin controls for environment management and usage analytics. Daily usage of Codex has grown by more than 10× since early August, and GPT-5-Codex served over 40 trillion tokens in just the three weeks since its launch as the model powering Codex. Real-world results at launch included Cisco engineers cutting code review times by 50%, and Instacart using the Codex SDK to automate tech debt cleanup and backlog reduction through their internal background coding agent platform. Inside OpenAI itself, nearly all engineers now use Codex and merge 70% more pull requests each week, with Codex automatically reviewing almost every PR to catch critical issues before they reach production.

title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5 in the API as Its Most Capable Coding and Agentic Model Yet
summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5 in its developer API on August 7, 2025, offering state-of-the-art coding performance alongside new controls for reasoning effort, verbosity, and custom tool formats.
content: OpenAI released GPT-5 in its API platform on August 7, 2025, positioning it as the company's strongest model for coding and agentic tasks, scoring 74.9% on SWE-bench Verified and 88% on the Aider polyglot coding benchmark — both new state-of-the-art results at time of release. The model ships in three sizes (gpt-5, gpt-5-mini, and gpt-5-nano) to give developers flexibility across cost and latency, with GPT-5 priced at $1.25 per million input tokens and $10 per million output tokens. New API features include a 'verbosity' parameter to control response length, a 'minimal' reasoning effort setting for latency-sensitive tasks, and support for custom tools that allow GPT-5 to call functions using plaintext rather than JSON — reducing escaping errors on long code inputs. GPT-5 makes roughly 80% fewer factual errors than o3 on LongFact benchmarks, and achieved 96.7% on the τ2-bench telecom tool-calling benchmark at a time when no prior model had exceeded 49%. Early testers Cursor, Windsurf, and Vercel all reported GPT-5 setting new internal records for coding quality and steerability.

title: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve Discovers New Math Algorithms and Speeds Up Gemini Training
summary: Google DeepMind unveiled AlphaEvolve, an evolutionary coding agent that combines Gemini LLMs with automated evaluators to discover novel algorithms across mathematics and computing infrastructure.
content: Google DeepMind announced AlphaEvolve on May 14, 2025 — an evolutionary coding agent that pairs Gemini Flash and Gemini Pro models with automated program evaluators to iteratively discover and optimize algorithms in mathematics and real-world computing systems. AlphaEvolve has already been deployed inside Google's infrastructure: it recovered 0.7% of Google's worldwide compute resources by improving data center scheduling in Borg, proposed a Verilog circuit rewrite that was integrated into an upcoming Tensor Processing Unit (TPU), and sped up a critical matrix multiplication kernel in Gemini's architecture by 23%, reducing total Gemini training time by 1%. In pure mathematics, the system improved upon the 1969 Strassen algorithm for 4×4 complex-valued matrix multiplication by reducing the required scalar multiplications from 49 to 48, and advanced the 300-year-old kissing number problem in 11 dimensions by discovering a configuration of 593 outer spheres. Across more than 50 open mathematical problems spanning geometry, combinatorics, and number theory, AlphaEvolve improved upon the previously best-known solutions in roughly 20% of cases. DeepMind is planning an Early Access Program for academic users and exploring broader availability of the system.

title: Microsoft Build 2025: Copilot Tuning Lets Enterprises Train AI on Their Own Data Without Code
summary: Microsoft unveiled Copilot Tuning and multi-agent orchestration at Build 2025, enabling organizations to customize AI models on proprietary data through a low-code interface in Copilot Studio.
content: At Microsoft Build 2025 on May 19, 2025, Microsoft announced Copilot Tuning — a capability in Copilot Studio that allows any organization to fine-tune AI models using its own proprietary data, workflows, and processes without requiring data scientists or custom infrastructure, with agents operating entirely within the Microsoft 365 service boundary. Multi-agent orchestration was also introduced, enabling multiple specialized Copilot agents to collaborate on complex business tasks — for example, coordinating HR, IT, and marketing agents together to onboard a new employee — while human oversight remains central to the workflow. More than 230,000 organizations, including 90% of the Fortune 500, already use Copilot Studio, and customers created over 1 million custom agents in the prior quarter alone — a 130% quarter-over-quarter increase. Additional platform updates included Microsoft Entra Agent ID for automatic identity and access management of AI agents, Purview Information Protection extensions for data governance, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for secure access to external data and tools. Deployments highlighted at Build included Wells Fargo cutting employee search time from 10 minutes to 30 seconds across 1,700 procedures for 35,000 bankers, and T-Mobile assembling real-time product information from more than 20 device manufacturers instantly.
